items.title: Remote NT communities appeal to governments for fuel security assurances, price relief as costs soar
items.summary: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

items.title: Six people killed in Kyiv after gunman opens fire and barricades supermarket
items.summary: At least six people have been killed and 14 are injured after a gunman opened fire in the streets of Kyiv.

items.title: Iran's reclosure of the Strait of Hormuz 'disappointing', Marles says
items.summary: The deputy prime minister says "every diplomatic angle" should be used to secure a permanent ceasefire in the Middle East.

items.title: Couple makes handmade skis out of Australian-grown timber in NSW Snowy Mountains
items.summary: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

items.title: Socceroos striker Mo Toure nets second Norwich hat-trick in English Championship
items.summary: Young Socceroo Mohamed Toure continues to shine in the lead-up to the World Cup, scoring another hat-trick for English Championship side Norwich.

items.title: Thousands of dollars worth of belongings stolen from cars shipped across Australia, but no-one held responsible
items.summary: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

items.title: Man faces court charged with culpable driving causing death after Melbourne pedestrian death
items.summary: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

items.title: Fantasy becomes reality for next-gen speculative fiction authors riding self-publishing boom
items.summary: Is your dream to become the next JK Rowling or Stephanie Meyer? A new wave of writers is turning to self-publishing to turn fantasy into reality.

items.title: Head coach of the Tasmania Devils Women's VFL side's tarot reading pastime
items.summary: Georgia Walker describes herself as a psychic medium, which she combines with her skills as a counsellor and teacher, as head coach of the Tasmania Devils' women's VFL team.

items.title: Fuel costs no obstacle as West Australian aurora chasers hit the road
items.summary: For Leonie and Dean McFaull, fuel prices aren't stopping them crossing the country to add to their capture of more than 100 auroras.

items.title: Some Royal Darwin Hospital staff use ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim
items.summary: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

items.title: World's largest live export ship sails out of Darwin with 17,000 cattle bound for Indonesia
items.summary: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

items.title: Young people taking initiative to quit at Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program
items.summary: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

items.title: Kimberley diphtheria rates more than triple in a month after 50-year eradication
items.summary: An outbreak of the long-dormant, highly contagious bacterial infection diphtheria in Western Australia's north is raising alarm among government officials and experts.

items.title: Liberal candidate under investigation for roadworks ahead of Nepean by-election
items.summary: The Liberal Party's candidate for the crucial upcoming Nepean by-election is being investigated by Victoria's transport department for unauthorised roadworks in a campaign stunt with Opposition Leader Jess Wilson.

items.title: Record earthquake near Cadia gold mine sparks calls for more monitors
items.summary: The magnitude 4.5-earthquake near Orange was logged as the region's largest on record, but only thanks to public monitoring data far away from the epicentre.

items.title: Women's shelters turn curated second-hand fashion into fundraising for frontlines services
items.summary: Women's shelters are having to get creative to acquire necessary funding such as turning fashion into sustaining frontline support.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate lodge amended defamation claim against critics posting on social media
items.summary: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

items.title: Adelaide Crows to give Jordan Dawson time before AFL return, Matthew Nicks says
items.summary: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

items.title: Pale octopus species mysteriously disappears from SA's Spencer Gulf
items.summary: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

items.title: School's move to restrict 'recognisable' photos of students on social media welcomed by criminologist
items.summary: The independent girls' school in Brisbane is now only sharing what it says are unidentifiable shots of students' faces from the side or behind, due to concerns about image manipulation.

items.title: Visits to NSW national parks hit record high with more young people heading outdoors, data shows
items.summary: The latest NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service Park Visitation Survey shows there were 65.5 million visits to the state's parks over the 2024-25 financial year.

items.title: 'Old bushie' Steve Hawe has a dream to make his board game an Aussie classic
items.summary: Steve Hawe says he never set out to make a board game — but when inspiration struck, he couldn't ignore it.

items.title: How statue of mythical sex worker 'Joy' celebrates industry
items.summary: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.

items.title: The Solomons Six were recruited to a 'catch and kill' squad in WWII — their stories were lost to history until now
items.summary: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

items.title: The Australians keeping a century of rainfall records as stations disappear
items.summary: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

items.title: NDIS unregistered provider crackdown coming, as Butler searches for more than $6b in savings
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

items.title: From celebration to calls of weakness, how the Trump-Pope Leo relationship soured
items.summary: US President Donald Trump described Pope Leo XIV's accession to the papacy as a meaningful moment for America. But now he has said the pontiff is hurting the Catholic Church, and is weak. Here's how their relationship has developed over the past year.

items.title: As the world races to shore up oil supplies, Japan promotes a stockpiling model to Asia
items.summary: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

items.title: Why Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration is raising eyebrows
items.summary: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

items.title: How the perfect mother myth was unravelled by a family mystery in Italy
items.summary: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

items.title: Today in history, April 19: Good News Week premiered 30 years ago
items.summary: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

items.title: Jessie Ware on how cowboys, sex and parenting shaped new album Superbloom
items.summary: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

items.title: Ready for Hard Quiz success? It's time to temper those expectations
items.summary: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

items.title: Japan, Australia sign deal for first three Mogami frigate warships
items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles and his Japanese counterpart, Koizumi Shinjirō, signed the deal aboard one of the warships docked in Melbourne.

items.title: Supanova Comic Con attendee killed after car strikes pedestrians in Melbourne
items.summary: A young man who was killed when a car struck a group of pedestrians in Melbourne's north-west was on his way home from attending the Supanova Comic Con & Gaming convention, police say.

items.title: Wallaroos beaten by USA in Pacific Four Series after Kansas City storm delay
items.summary: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

items.title: Alleged arson attacks and shootings in Sydney prompt fears of escalating violence
items.summary: NSW Police has expressed concern about escalating violence between Sydney's organised crime networks, following several alleged arson attacks and public shootings overnight.

items.title: Dowerin buzzing as Lance 'Buddy' Franklin returns to footy for childhood club
items.summary: Lance Franklin has pulled on the boots to help the Dowerin Tigers, where he played his formative footy, raise much-needed funds for lights and facilities.

items.title: Power price hikes threaten Northern Territory councils, may cut access to services like pools and street lights
items.summary: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

items.title: Australian tea tree oil industry faces uncertainty over EU ban proposal
items.summary: The EU is considering banning Australian tea tree oil after a study raised concerns about potential reproductive risks, a move industry leaders warn could devastate exports and trigger global impacts.

items.title: Mushroom murderer's estranged husband Simon Patterson writing memoir
items.summary: Simon Patterson, who claims his murderous ex-wife tried to poison him on several occasions, is pursuing a book deal, his media manager has announced.

items.title: Haas expected to miss several weeks as Broncos count cost of brave win
items.summary: Brisbane's horror run of injuries has continued with Payne Haas limping off in the first half of the Broncos' one-point win over Wests Tigers.

items.title: India's parliament rejects move to increase number of women in office
items.summary: The largest democracy in the world has rejected a move to increase the number of women in parliament. What could have been a 'beacon' for global women's rights became marred by controversy.

items.title: Indemnified soldiers give evidence in Ben Roberts-Smith case, documents show
items.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith was on Friday granted bail in the NSW Local Court, with his case over war crimes charges expected to return to court in early June.

items.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval
items.summary: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

items.title: Skip the takeaway. This halloumi wrap is quicker and healthy too - ABC News
items.summary: The only cooking in this simple dinner is the halloumi. It has takeaway vibes and just the right balance of flavours to become a family favourite.

items.title: How Maltese migrants united to shape Mackay's sugarcane legacy
items.summary: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

items.title: Death of Sandipan Dhar at Joondalup Health Campus was 'probably preventable'
items.summary: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

items.title: Agricultural, transport industries call for urgent road repairs in flood-hit Northern Territory
items.summary: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

items.title: Social croquet connects communities as clubs record jump in numbers
items.summary: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

items.title: Marie-Louise Eta takes charge of Union Berlin as first female head coach in Europe's big five leagues
items.summary: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

items.title: How the daughters of WA mining pioneers came to be opponents in high-profile legal fight
items.summary: Their fathers were school buddies turned mining pioneers, and in another life they could have been friends — but Gina Rinehart and Angela Bennett came to be opponents in one of the most high-stakes legal battles Australia has ever seen.

items.title: Do acupuncture and herbs actually help with menopause symptoms?
items.summary: Complementary therapies may help some menopausal symptoms, but more high-quality research is needed to understand how effective and safe these treatments truly are.

items.title: FIFA World Cup fans set for $200 train tickets for 15 minute trip to MetLife Stadium
items.summary: A 15-minute round-trip train fare from New York to New Jersey's MetLife Stadium will set World Cup football fans back more than $200 as officials struggle to cover rising costs.

items.title: Producer backs pitch for major film studio in Port Kembla
items.summary: As the New South Wales government searches for a site for a second Sydney film studio, one of Australia's most prominent producers suggests a regional location is the ideal fit.

items.title: Frustrated parents of under-16s look for help to enforce 'ineffective' social media ban
items.summary: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

items.title: Lime scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means for commuters
items.summary: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

items.title: How one walk could spark a national conversation on Australia's past
items.summary: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

items.title: Chris Minns considers his options after court ruling over NSW protest laws
items.summary: NSW Premier Chris Minns must now decide whether to proceed with the next restrictions he hoped to impose on protests after his latest court defeat.

items.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through orchestral music in Adelaide
items.summary: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

items.title: Missing votes bungle caps off disastrous poll for SA's electoral commission
items.summary: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

items.title: How artificial intelligence is changing business interaction with customers
items.summary: Businesses say they are using AI to speed up service delivery and improve the customer experience, but economists warn this may be the "calm before the storm".

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith prosecution for alleged war crimes complex, legal expert says
items.summary: A legal expert says the criminal prosecution of former soldier Ben Roberts-Smith will test Australia's war crimes legal framework in ways never seen before, as the case moves toward a trial.

items.title: Calls for more awareness of functional neurological disorder to reduce diagnosis delays
items.summary: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.
